speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Stand forth, Dame Eleanor Cobham, Gloucester’s wife.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: In sight of God and us, your guilt is great.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Receive the sentence of the law for sins

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Such as by God’s book are adjudged to death.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: You four, from hence to prison back again;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: From thence unto the place of execution:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The witch in Smithfield shall be burnt to ashes,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And you three shall be strangled on the gallows.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text:
To
                 
                Duchess
                 
              You, madam, for you are more nobly born,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Despoilèd of your honor in your life,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall, after three days’ open penance done,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Live in your country here in banishment

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With Sir John Stanley in the Isle of Man.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Welcome is banishment. Welcome were my death.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Eleanor, the law, thou seest, hath judged thee.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I cannot justify whom the law condemns.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Mine eyes are full of tears, my heart of grief.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ah, Humphrey, this dishonor in thine age

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Will bring thy head with sorrow to the ground.—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I beseech your Majesty give me leave to go;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sorrow would solace, and mine age would ease.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Stay, Humphrey, Duke of Gloucester. Ere thou go,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Give up thy staff. Henry will to himself

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Protector be; and God shall be my hope,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My stay, my guide, and lantern to my feet.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And go in peace, Humphrey, no less beloved

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Than when thou wert Protector to thy king.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I see no reason why a king of years

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Should be to be protected like a child.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: God and King Henry govern England’s realm!—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Give up your staff, sir, and the King his realm.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: My staff?—Here, noble Henry, is my staff.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As willingly do I the same resign

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As e’er thy father Henry made it mine;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And even as willingly at thy feet I leave it

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As others would ambitiously receive it.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Farewell, good king. When I am dead and gone,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: May honorable peace attend thy throne.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Why, now is Henry king and Margaret queen,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And Humphrey, Duke of Gloucester, scarce himself,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That bears so shrewd a maim. Two pulls at once:

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: His lady banished and a limb lopped off.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: This staff of honor raught, there let it stand

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Where it best fits to be, in Henry’s hand.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thus droops this lofty pine and hangs his sprays;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thus Eleanor’s pride dies in her youngest days.

speaker: YORK
text: Lords, let him go.—Please it your Majesty,

speaker: YORK
text: This is the day appointed for the combat,

speaker: YORK
text: And ready are the appellant and defendant—

speaker: YORK
text: The armorer and his man—to enter the lists,

speaker: YORK
text: So please your Highness to behold the fight.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ay, good my lord, for purposely therefor

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Left I the court to see this quarrel tried.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I’ God’s name, see the lists and all things fit.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Here let them end it, and God defend the right!

speaker: YORK
text: I never saw a fellow worse bestead

speaker: YORK
text: Or more afraid to fight than is the appellant,

speaker: YORK
text: The servant of this armorer, my lords.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              NEIGHBOR
text: Here, neighbor Horner, I drink to you

speaker:
FIRST
               
              NEIGHBOR
text: in a cup of sack; and fear not, neighbor, you shall

speaker:
FIRST
               
              NEIGHBOR
text: do well enough.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              NEIGHBOR
text: And here, neighbor, here’s a cup of

speaker:
SECOND
               
              NEIGHBOR
text: charneco.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              NEIGHBOR
text: And here’s a pot of good double beer,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              NEIGHBOR
text: neighbor. Drink, and fear not your man.

speaker: HORNER
text: Let it come, i’ faith, and I’ll pledge you all.

speaker: HORNER
text: And a fig for Peter!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PRENTICE
text: Here, Peter, I drink to thee, and be not

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PRENTICE
text: afraid.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              PRENTICE
text: Be merry, Peter, and fear not thy

speaker:
SECOND
               
              PRENTICE
text: master. Fight for credit of the prentices.

speaker: PETER
text: I thank you all. Drink, and pray for me, I pray

speaker: PETER
text: you, for I think I have taken my last draft in this

speaker: PETER
text: world. Here, Robin, an if I die, I give thee my

speaker: PETER
text: apron.—And, Will, thou shalt have my hammer.—

speaker: PETER
text: And here, Tom, take all the money that I have.

speaker: PETER
text: O Lord, bless me, I

speaker: PETER
text: pray God, for I am never able to deal with my

speaker: PETER
text: master. He hath learnt so much fence already.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Come, leave your drinking, and fall to

speaker: SALISBURY
text: blows. Sirrah, what’s thy name?

speaker: PETER
text: Peter, forsooth.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Peter? What more?

speaker: PETER
text: Thump.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Thump? Then see thou thump thy master

speaker: SALISBURY
text: well.

speaker: HORNER
text: Masters, I am come hither, as it were, upon

speaker: HORNER
text: my man’s instigation, to prove him a knave and

speaker: HORNER
text: myself an honest man; and touching the Duke of

speaker: HORNER
text: York, I will take my death I never meant him any

speaker: HORNER
text: ill, nor the King, nor the Queen.—And therefore,

speaker: HORNER
text: Peter, have at thee with a downright blow!

speaker: YORK
text: Dispatch. This knave’s tongue begins to double.

speaker: YORK
text: Sound, trumpets. Alarum to the combatants!

speaker: HORNER
text: Hold, Peter, hold! I confess, I confess treason.

speaker: YORK
text: Take away his weapon.—Fellow, thank God and

speaker: YORK
text: the good wine in thy master’s way.

speaker: PETER
text: O God, have I overcome mine enemies in this

speaker: PETER
text: presence? O Peter, thou hast prevailed in right!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Go, take hence that traitor from our sight;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For by his death we do perceive his guilt.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And God in justice hath revealed to us

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The truth and innocence of this poor fellow,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Which he had thought to have murdered wrongfully.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come, fellow, follow us for thy reward.
